fact: March 20 is almost always the spring equinox in the Northern Hemisphere, which means day and night are nearly equal lengths - like nature's way of splitting time perfectly in half!
On this date, the sun rises almost exactly due east and sets almost exactly due west, turning Earth into a giant compass!
In Antarctica, March 20 marks the beginning of six months of darkness - imagine not seeing the sun until September!
Tree sap starts flowing like crazy around this time in maple trees across North America, which is why maple syrup season peaks right now!
Bears in northern regions are typically waking up from hibernation around March 20, stumbling out of their dens super hungry after months of sleeping!
The zodiac sign switches from Pisces to Aries on March 20, making it one of only 12 days each year when the astrological sign changes!
Migrating birds use this date as nature's alarm clock - millions of species start their epic journeys north around the spring equinox!
In Japan, cherry blossom season often explodes around this time, with some trees blooming so fast you can literally watch the flowers open!
March 20 sits at day 79 or 80 of the year (depending on leap years), making it the point where you've completed about 22% of the entire year!
Earthworms often become super active around this date, squirming up to the surface in large numbers as the soil warms up in northern climates!
In the Southern Hemisphere, March 20 kicks off autumn, meaning kids in Australia are experiencing fall weather while kids in Canada are welcoming spring!
